DECLARATION OF PHILLIP A COOKE IN SUPPORT OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF MESCHER, DAN INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA DANIEL'S CONSTRUCTION

DECLARATION OF DAN MESCHER IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO DEFT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF MESCHER, DAN INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA DANIEL'S CONSTRUCTION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PACIFIC, GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTS THE BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE COMMON INTEREST PRIVILEGE APPLIES. (CIVIL CODE SECTION 47(C)). PLAINTIFF DOES NOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING MALICE. "ACTUAL MALICE" IS ESTABLISHED BY A SHOWING THAT THE PUBLICATION WAS MOTIVATED BY HATRED OR ILL WILL TOWARD PLAINTIFF OR BY A SHOWING THAT DEFENDANT LACKED REASONABLE GROUNDS FOR BELIEF IN THE TRUTH OF THE PUBLICATION AND THEREFORE ACTED IN RECKLESS DISREGARD OF PLAINTIFF'S RIGHTS. (NOEL VS. RIVERHILLS WILSONS, INC. (2003) 113 CAL. APP. 4TH 1363, 1370.) HERE, PLAINTIFF SHOWS NEITHER. THE COURT ADOPTS DEFENDANT'S PROPOSED ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR 5759

AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-12-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PACIFIC, GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/or in the alternative summary adjudication. Appearances: Philip A. Cooke, Law Offices of Philip A. Cooke, (530) 671-1100, Appearing thru CourtCall, for Plaintiff and opposing party; Amber Rye Brumfiel, Esq. of Sedgwick Detert, Oran & Arnold LLP (415) 781-7900, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Summary Judgment granted. Defendant shifts the burden of proof. Plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue of fact. The common interest privilege applies. (Civil Code section 47(c)). Plaintiff does not create a triable issue of fact regarding malice. "Actual Malice" is established by a showing that the publication was motivated by hatred or ill will toward plaintiff or by a showing that defendant lacked reasonable grounds for belief in the truth of the publication and therefore acted in reckless disregard of plaintiff's rights. (Noel vs. RiverHills Wilsons, Inc. (2003) 113 Cal. App. 4th 1363, 1370.) Here, plaintiff shows neither. The Court adopts defendant's proposed order. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
